channel.title: Sensa Wellness Blog
channel.description: Expert guides on inflammation, chronic disease, diet, lifestyle, and at-home CRP testing from Sensa Wellness.

item.title: Gallbladder Inflammation (Cholecystitis): Causes, CRP, and What Helps
item.description: Cholecystitis is inflammation of the gallbladder, usually triggered by gallstones. Learn how it develops, why CRP rises sharply, and what helps calm the inflammation.

item.title: Pancreatitis and Inflammation: How the Pancreas Turns on Itself
item.description: Pancreatitis is inflammation of the pancreas driven by its own digestive enzymes. Learn how it develops, why CRP tracks severity, and what protects the pancreas.

item.title: IBS and Inflammation: Is Irritable Bowel Syndrome Inflammatory?
item.description: IBS is not classically inflammatory, but research reveals low-grade immune activation, mast cells, and gut-brain signaling. Here is what inflammation means for IBS.

item.title: SIBO and Inflammation: How Bacterial Overgrowth Drives Gut Inflammation
item.description: SIBO is an overgrowth of bacteria in the small intestine that can drive gut inflammation, damage the barrier, and provoke immune activation. Here is how it works.

item.title: Hashimoto's and Inflammation: The Autoimmune Attack on the Thyroid
item.description: Hashimoto's thyroiditis is an autoimmune condition in which the immune system inflames and gradually destroys the thyroid gland. Here is how that inflammation works.

item.title: Psoriatic Arthritis and Inflammation: When Skin and Joints Both Flare
item.description: Psoriatic arthritis links inflamed skin and inflamed joints through a shared immune pathway. Here is how the IL-17 and IL-23 axis drives disease in both.

item.title: Ankylosing Spondylitis and Inflammation: Understanding Inflammatory Back Pain
item.description: Ankylosing spondylitis is an inflammatory arthritis of the spine driven by HLA-B27 and the IL-23 and IL-17 axis. Here is what separates inflammatory back pain from ordinary back pain.

item.title: Chronic Hives and Inflammation: What Drives Recurrent Urticaria
item.description: Chronic hives are driven by mast cells releasing histamine into the skin. Here is how that inflammatory process works and why it can persist for months or years.

item.title: Stroke and Inflammation: How Inflammation Raises Stroke Risk
item.description: Inflammation shapes stroke risk before an event and drives brain damage after one. Learn how CRP predicts risk and how post-stroke neuroinflammation affects recovery.

item.title: Atherosclerosis and Inflammation: Why Plaque Is an Inflammatory Disease
item.description: Atherosclerosis was long seen as passive cholesterol buildup. Modern science shows it is an inflammatory disease, from LDL oxidation to the CANTOS trial and CRP.

item.title: Anemia of Inflammation: How Chronic Inflammation Causes Low Iron
item.description: Anemia of inflammation is caused by chronic inflammation, not iron deficiency. Learn how hepcidin, IL-6, and ferritin explain why iron gets locked away from the body.

item.title: Hair Loss and Inflammation: The Inflammatory Roots of Shedding
item.description: Many kinds of hair loss share an inflammatory root. Learn how scalp inflammation, alopecia areata autoimmunity, and telogen effluvium drive shedding, and where CRP fits.

item.title: The Ketogenic Diet and Inflammation: Does Keto Lower CRP?
item.description: Does the ketogenic diet lower inflammation? The evidence on keto and CRP is mixed, but the ketone BHB has genuine anti-inflammatory power. Here is what the science shows.

item.title: Vaping and Inflammation: What E-Cigarettes Do to Your Body
item.description: E-cigarettes are marketed as safer than smoking, but vaping drives oxidative stress, endothelial dysfunction, and airway and systemic inflammation. Here is what the science shows.

item.title: Yoga and Inflammation: How a Mind-Body Practice Lowers CRP
item.description: Yoga does more than improve flexibility. Studies show it lowers IL-6, CRP, and cortisol through effects on the HPA axis and the vagus nerve. Here is how the science works.

item.title: Menopause and Inflammation: How Hormonal Change Raises Inflammatory Risk
item.description: The estrogen decline of menopause removes an anti-inflammatory brake, raising IL-6, TNF-alpha, CRP, and visceral fat. Here is how hormonal change reshapes inflammatory risk.

item.title: CRP and Heart Attack Risk: What Your Inflammation Number Predicts
item.description: High-sensitivity CRP is an independent predictor of heart attack and stroke risk. Learn what the below 1, 1 to 3, and above 3 mg/L cutoffs mean for your heart.

item.title: hsCRP vs Standard CRP: What Is the Difference?
item.description: hsCRP and standard CRP measure the same protein but at different ranges. Learn why high-sensitivity CRP is used for cardiovascular risk and standard CRP for infection.

item.title: CRP and Cholesterol: Why Inflammation Matters as Much as LDL
item.description: Cholesterol and inflammation are two separate drivers of heart disease. Learn why CRP adds risk information beyond LDL and why both numbers matter for prevention.

item.title: CRP After Surgery: What Is Normal and When to Worry
item.description: CRP rises sharply after surgery, peaking around 2 to 3 days, then falls. Learn what the normal pattern looks like and why a secondary rise can signal infection.

item.title: Inflammaging: How Chronic Inflammation Accelerates the Aging Process
item.description: Learn about inflammaging, how low-grade chronic inflammation speeds up aging and increases disease risk. Monitor your inflammation at home with Sensa.

item.title: Air Pollution and Inflammation: The Invisible Threat to Your Immune System
item.description: Particulate matter and toxic gases from air pollution activate inflammatory pathways in the lungs and bloodstream. Here is what the research shows about the invisible link between the air you breathe and chronic disease.

item.title: Alcohol and Inflammation: The Science Behind the Immune Disruption
item.description: Alcohol's effects on inflammation are complex, dose-dependent, and frequently misrepresented. Here is what the research actually shows about drinking and your immune system.

item.title: Allergies and Inflammation: Your Immune System's Overreaction
item.description: Allergies trigger an inflammatory immune response that can affect your whole body. Learn how Sensa helps you track inflammation levels during allergy season.

item.title: Alzheimer's Disease and Neuroinflammation: New Evidence for an Old Enemy
item.description: The amyloid hypothesis of Alzheimer's disease is being revised to incorporate neuroinflammation as a central driver, not just a bystander. Here is what the latest evidence shows about microglia, APOE4, and lifestyle prevention.

item.title: The Anti-Inflammatory Diet: What the Science Actually Says
item.description: From the PREDIMED trial to omega-3 eicosanoids and gut microbiome science, here is what the evidence actually supports about anti-inflammatory eating.

item.title: Anxiety and Inflammation: The Bidirectional Relationship Linking Mental and Physical Health
item.description: Anxiety disorders are associated with elevated CRP and IL-6. Anti-inflammatory interventions reduce anxiety symptoms in clinical trials. Here is the biology connecting fear pathways and immune activation.

item.title: Arthritis and Inflammation: Why Your Joints Are Telling You Something Important
item.description: Discover the role inflammation plays in arthritis and joint pain. Learn how monitoring CRP levels at home with Sensa can help you manage flare-ups proactively.

item.title: Autoimmune Disease and Inflammation: When the Immune System Turns on Itself
item.description: In autoimmune conditions, inflammation is not a response to a pathogen. It is a misdirected attack on the body's own tissues. Understanding what drives this immune dysregulation is central to managing it.

item.title: Autophagy and Inflammation: How Cellular Self-Cleaning Prevents Chronic Disease
item.description: Autophagy removes the damaged proteins, dysfunctional mitochondria, and cellular debris that trigger inflammasome activation and chronic inflammation. Here is how to support it.

item.title: Go Beyond the Scale: 5 Wellness Markers That Matter More Than Weight
item.description: Weight is just one number. Discover 5 wellness markers that give a fuller picture of your health, including inflammation, sleep, and body composition.

item.title: Inflammation and Bone Loss: The Hidden Driver of Osteoporosis
item.description: Chronic inflammation disrupts the balance between bone formation and bone breakdown, quietly driving osteoporosis. Here's what the research shows and what you can do about it.

item.title: Neuroinflammation: How Chronic Inflammation Affects Your Brain and Cognitive Health
item.description: Chronic systemic inflammation breaches the blood-brain barrier and activates microglia, contributing to cognitive decline, Alzheimer's, and brain fog.

item.title: The Inflammation-Cancer Connection: What Research Reveals
item.description: Explore the research linking chronic inflammation to cancer development and progression. Learn why monitoring your inflammation markers matters for long-term health.

item.title: Chronic Pain and Inflammation: The Cycle That Keeps You Stuck
item.description: Chronic pain and inflammation reinforce each other through central sensitization and neuroinflammatory mechanisms. Here is what the research shows about breaking this cycle.

item.title: Circadian Rhythm and Inflammation: Why Your Body Clock Controls Your Immune System
item.description: The circadian clock directly regulates inflammatory gene expression. Disrupting your body clock through shift work, late eating, or irregular sleep elevates CRP and raises chronic disease risk.

item.title: Cold Exposure and Inflammation: The Science Behind Cold Plunges and Ice Baths
item.description: Cold water immersion activates norepinephrine release and hormetic stress responses that reduce systemic inflammation. Here is what the clinical evidence shows about protocols, benefits, and who benefits most.

item.title: COPD and Inflammation: Understanding the Lung-Immune Connection
item.description: Understand the link between COPD and systemic inflammation. See how tracking CRP levels with Sensa can help you stay ahead of flare-ups and lung health changes.

item.title: Curcumin and Inflammation: Does Turmeric Actually Work?
item.description: Curcumin inhibits NF-kB and reduces CRP in clinical trials. But the bioavailability problem is real. Here is what the evidence shows about which forms work, what doses matter, and who benefits most.

item.title: The Inflammation-Depression Connection: Is Your Immune System Affecting Your Mood?
item.description: Emerging research reveals that chronic inflammation may drive depression through cytokine signaling, microglial activation, and neuroinflammation pathways.

item.title: Inflammation and Diabetes: The Connection You Need to Understand
item.description: Learn how chronic inflammation contributes to insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes, and how tracking your CRP levels with Sensa can help you take control.

item.title: Eczema and Inflammation: The Immune Imbalance Behind Atopic Dermatitis
item.description: Atopic dermatitis is driven by Th2 immune skewing and skin barrier dysfunction that creates a cycle of inflammation, itching, and barrier damage. Here is what the research shows about its systemic connections.

item.title: Endometriosis and Inflammation: Why Immune Dysfunction Is Central to This Condition
item.description: Endometriosis is fundamentally a disease of immune dysregulation in which the peritoneal environment fails to clear ectopic endometrial tissue. Chronic peritoneal inflammation drives lesion growth and systemic symptoms.

item.title: Estrogen and Inflammation: Why Menopause Changes Your Inflammatory Profile
item.description: Estrogen suppresses NF-kB activity and promotes anti-inflammatory immune regulation. The menopausal drop in estrogen removes this protection, explaining the sharp rise in cardiovascular and inflammatory disease risk after menopause.

item.title: Exercise and Inflammation: Why Movement Is the Best Anti-Inflammatory Medicine
item.description: Exercise temporarily raises inflammatory markers, yet regular exercisers have the lowest chronic inflammation. Understanding this paradox is key to using movement as medicine.

item.title: Exercise, Recovery, and Inflammation: How to Train Smarter
item.description: Exercise triggers a brief inflammatory response that drives adaptation. But too much training without adequate recovery can tip into chronic inflammation. Learn how to balance the two.

item.title: Fertility and Inflammation: How Chronic Immune Activation Affects Reproductive Health
item.description: Chronic inflammation impairs ovarian reserve, disrupts implantation, reduces sperm quality, and increases miscarriage risk through well-characterized immunological mechanisms.

item.title: Dietary Fiber and Inflammation: Why Your Gut Bacteria Need You to Eat More Plants
item.description: Dietary fiber feeds gut bacteria that produce short-chain fatty acids, which strengthen the gut barrier and directly suppress NF-kB signaling. High-fiber diets consistently reduce CRP in clinical trials.

item.title: Fibromyalgia and Inflammation: Rethinking Widespread Pain
item.description: Fibromyalgia has long been considered a non-inflammatory condition. New research on neuroinflammation and central sensitization is fundamentally changing that view.

item.title: GLP-1 Medications and Inflammation: What You Should Know
item.description: GLP-1 medications like Ozempic may reduce inflammation alongside weight loss. Learn how CRP tracking helps you understand your body's response to any treatment.

item.title: Gum Disease and Systemic Inflammation: The Mouth-Body Connection
item.description: Periodontitis is the most common chronic infectious disease in humans and one of the most underappreciated drivers of systemic inflammation. The bacteria in your gums affect your heart, blood sugar, and brain.

item.title: Gut Health and Inflammation: How Your Microbiome Controls Systemic Immunity
item.description: Your gut microbiome plays a central role in regulating inflammation throughout your body. Learn how gut health impacts CRP levels and how to track changes at home.

item.title: How Inflammation Drives Heart Disease: Beyond Cholesterol
item.description: Cholesterol is only part of the story. Discover how chronic inflammation damages arteries and drives heart disease, and why CRP monitoring is a key health tool.

item.title: Hydration and Inflammation: The Overlooked Connection
item.description: Chronic mild dehydration elevates inflammatory markers and impairs the body's ability to clear inflammatory waste. Learn how hydration status affects CRP and systemic inflammation.

item.title: Hypertension and Inflammation: The Vascular Link Between Pressure and Immune Activation
item.description: High blood pressure and chronic inflammation reinforce each other through endothelial dysfunction, oxidative stress, and renin-angiotensin system activation. Here is the bidirectional biology.

item.title: IBD and Inflammation: The Science Behind Crohn's Disease and Ulcerative Colitis
item.description: Inflammatory bowel disease involves dysregulated mucosal immune responses in the gut with systemic consequences. Here is the immunological biology, the gut microbiome role, and what drives flares and remission.

item.title: Inflammation: The Common Thread Behind Nearly Every Chronic Disease
item.description: Chronic inflammation is linked to heart disease, diabetes, cancer, and more. Learn why it is the root cause of most chronic diseases and how to monitor it.

item.title: The Top Inflammatory Foods: What to Reduce and Why
item.description: Trans fats, refined carbohydrates, ultra-processed foods, and certain cooking methods each activate inflammatory pathways through distinct mechanisms. Here is the evidence behind the most reliably pro-inflammatory dietary patterns.

item.title: Physical Injuries and Inflammation: When Healing Goes Too Far
item.description: Understand why inflammation after an injury can become chronic and delay recovery. Track your body's healing response with at-home CRP monitoring from Sensa.

item.title: Intermittent Fasting and Inflammation: What the Research Actually Shows
item.description: Intermittent fasting reduces inflammatory markers in multiple clinical trials. Here is what happens metabolically during the fasted state, and how time-restricted eating differs from extended fasting.

item.title: Kidney Disease and Chronic Inflammation: A Dangerous Feedback Loop
item.description: The kidneys both regulate and are damaged by inflammatory processes. In chronic kidney disease, this relationship becomes a vicious cycle that accelerates organ decline and systemic disease.

item.title: Liver Inflammation and Metabolic Disease: The Silent Epidemic of NAFLD
item.description: The liver is the body's primary filter for inflammatory signals. When it becomes chronically inflamed, the effects ripple through every organ system. Here is what you need to know about NAFLD and hepatic inflammation.

item.title: The Biology of Loneliness: How Social Isolation Triggers Chronic Inflammation
item.description: Loneliness activates a specific inflammatory gene expression pattern that evolved as a survival response. In modern life, chronic social isolation sustains this pattern in ways that directly damage health.

item.title: Long COVID and Inflammation: What Persistent Immune Activation Tells Us
item.description: Long COVID affects tens of millions of people globally. The emerging evidence points to persistent viral reservoirs, autoimmune activation, and chronic neuroinflammation as the primary drivers of prolonged symptoms.

item.title: Inflammation and Longevity: What the Science of Long Life Reveals
item.description: The science of longevity increasingly points to inflammation control as a central mechanism. Learn what blue zone populations, centenarian studies, and longevity research reveal about keeping inflammation low for a longer, healthier life.

item.title: ME/CFS and Inflammation: When the Immune System Fails to Reset
item.description: Myalgic encephalomyelitis and chronic fatigue syndrome involve persistent neuroinflammation and immune dysregulation. Learn what the latest research reveals about the inflammatory biology of ME/CFS.

item.title: Meditation, Mindfulness, and Inflammation: The Mind-Body Science
item.description: Mindfulness-based interventions reduce CRP and pro-inflammatory cytokines in multiple clinical trials. Here is the neuroimmunology behind how mental practice changes immune function.

item.title: The Mediterranean Diet and Inflammation: The Best-Studied Anti-Inflammatory Eating Pattern
item.description: The Mediterranean diet reduces CRP by up to 41 percent in randomized trials. Here is what makes it work, what the PREDIMED trial showed, and how to adopt it practically.

item.title: Metabolic Syndrome and Inflammation: The Five-Factor Cluster Behind Chronic Disease
item.description: Metabolic syndrome links abdominal obesity, high blood sugar, high triglycerides, low HDL, and high blood pressure through a shared inflammatory mechanism. Here is the biology connecting them.

item.title: Microplastics and Inflammation: An Emerging Environmental Threat to Immune Health
item.description: Microplastics and nanoplastics have been detected in human blood, lungs, liver, placenta, and arterial plaques. Here is what the evidence shows about their inflammatory effects and what you can do to reduce exposure.

item.title: Mitochondria and Inflammation: How Your Cellular Power Plants Control Immune Response
item.description: Damaged mitochondria release pro-inflammatory signals that activate the NLRP3 inflammasome and cGAS-STING pathway. Mitochondrial health is inseparable from inflammatory health.

item.title: Multiple Sclerosis and Neuroinflammation: What Goes Wrong in the Immune System
item.description: Multiple sclerosis is driven by T-cell-mediated neuroinflammation that destroys the myelin sheath. Here is the immunological biology behind the disease and what emerging research shows about modifiable inflammatory factors.

item.title: Noise Pollution and Inflammation: The Overlooked Environmental Health Risk
item.description: Chronic noise exposure activates the stress axis and disrupts sleep in ways that chronically elevate CRP and cardiovascular inflammatory markers. Here is what the evidence shows about traffic noise, health outcomes, and protection.

item.title: Obesity and Inflammation: The Bidirectional Relationship Fueling Chronic Disease
item.description: Excess adipose tissue is an active endocrine organ that continuously secretes inflammatory signals. This is why obesity and chronic disease are so closely linked.

item.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids and Inflammation: What the Clinical Research Shows
item.description: Omega-3 fatty acids have one of the strongest evidence bases of any dietary intervention for reducing systemic inflammation. Here is what the clinical research shows.

item.title: Parkinson's Disease and Neuroinflammation: The Gut-Brain Axis Connection
item.description: Neuroinflammation and microglial activation are central to Parkinson's disease progression. Emerging evidence suggests the disease may begin in the gut years before motor symptoms appear.

item.title: PCOS and Inflammation: The Hormonal Disorder with an Inflammatory Root
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome involves insulin resistance, androgen excess, and chronic low-grade inflammation that reinforce each other. Here is the biology and what anti-inflammatory strategies show in clinical research.

item.title: Polyphenols and Inflammation: The Plant Compounds with Real Anti-Inflammatory Evidence
item.description: Polyphenols from berries, olive oil, green tea, and dark chocolate have well-documented anti-inflammatory effects. Here is what the research shows about which compounds work best and why bioavailability matters.

item.title: Ultra-Processed Foods and Inflammation: Why the Modern Diet Is an Inflammatory One
item.description: Ultra-processed foods contain emulsifiers, preservatives, and additives that disrupt the gut barrier and microbiome, raising CRP and driving chronic disease independently of their poor nutritional profile.

item.title: Psoriasis and Systemic Inflammation: More Than a Skin Condition
item.description: Psoriasis is a systemic inflammatory disease that raises cardiovascular risk, worsens metabolic health, and drives joint destruction. Here is the biology behind why skin disease has whole-body consequences.

item.title: Red Meat and Inflammation: Separating the Science from the Headlines
item.description: The relationship between red meat and inflammation is more nuanced than most headlines suggest. Here is what the evidence actually shows about saturated fat, processed meat, TMAO, and inflammatory markers.

item.title: Resveratrol and Inflammation: The Red Wine Compound Under the Microscope
item.description: Resveratrol activates SIRT1 and inhibits NF-kB in animal models with impressive results. Human trials have been more mixed. Here is what the evidence actually shows.

item.title: Sarcopenia and Inflammation: Why You Lose Muscle as You Age
item.description: Chronic inflammation is one of the primary drivers of age-related muscle loss. Learn what sarcopenia is, how inflammation accelerates it, and what you can do about it.

item.title: Sauna and Inflammation: Why Heat Therapy Rivals Exercise as an Anti-Inflammatory Tool
item.description: Regular sauna use reduces CRP, IL-6, and cardiovascular mortality with effect sizes comparable to moderate aerobic exercise. Here is the biology behind the Finnish research and what protocols work best.

item.title: Sitting Is the New Smoking: How a Sedentary Lifestyle Fuels Chronic Inflammation
item.description: Prolonged sitting activates inflammatory pathways independently of total exercise volume. Even people who meet weekly exercise guidelines show elevated CRP if they sit for most of the day.

item.title: Silent Inflammation: The Hidden Driver Behind Chronic Disease
item.description: Silent inflammation has no obvious symptoms but fuels chronic disease over time. Learn how to detect it early with at-home CRP testing from Sensa Wellness.

item.title: How Inflammation Ages Your Skin: The Science of Inflammaging from the Outside In
item.description: Skin aging is more than sun damage and genetics. Chronic systemic inflammation accelerates every visible sign of aging from within. Here is what the research shows.

item.title: Sleep Apnea and Chronic Inflammation: Why Disrupted Breathing Inflames Your Body
item.description: Obstructive sleep apnea causes repeated episodes of low oxygen that activate systemic inflammatory pathways. Here is how intermittent hypoxia drives CRP elevation and cardiovascular risk beyond what poor sleep alone explains.

item.title: How Poor Sleep Fuels Chronic Inflammation, and What to Do About It
item.description: Sleep deprivation raises CRP and IL-6 within days. Learn how the sleep-inflammation feedback loop works and practical strategies to break the cycle.

item.title: Smoking, Vaping, and Inflammation: How Tobacco Activates Chronic Disease Pathways
item.description: Smoking is one of the most potent drivers of systemic chronic inflammation known to medicine. Here is the molecular biology behind why, and what happens to inflammation after quitting.

item.title: Stress and Inflammation: How Your Mind Triggers Your Immune System
item.description: Chronic psychological stress raises CRP, IL-6, and TNF-alpha in ways that parallel infection. Learn the biology behind stress-driven inflammation and how to interrupt it.

item.title: Sugar and Inflammation: The Sweet Truth About Dietary Glucose and Immune Activation
item.description: Excess dietary sugar elevates CRP through multiple distinct pathways: postprandial NF-kB activation, fructose-driven liver inflammation, and advanced glycation end product accumulation. Here is the complete picture.

item.title: Testosterone and Inflammation: The Hormonal Link Between Low T and Chronic Disease
item.description: Testosterone has direct anti-inflammatory effects on immune cells and vasculature. Low testosterone is consistently associated with elevated CRP and IL-6, and the relationship is bidirectional.

item.title: Thyroid Disease and Inflammation: The Autoimmune Connection Most People Miss
item.description: Hashimoto's thyroiditis is the most common cause of hypothyroidism, and it is fundamentally an inflammatory autoimmune disease. Understanding the inflammation-thyroid connection changes how the condition should be managed.

item.title: Understanding Your CRP: What the Numbers Actually Mean
item.description: CRP is the most widely used marker of systemic inflammation. Learn what different CRP levels mean, what can cause them to rise, and how to interpret trends over time.

item.title: Vitamin D and Inflammation: The Deficiency Most People Do Not Know They Have
item.description: Vitamin D functions as an immune-regulating hormone. Deficiency is widespread and consistently associated with elevated inflammatory markers. Here is what the research shows about supplementation and inflammation.

item.title: Can Weather Affect Your Inflammation? What the Research Shows
item.description: Cold temperatures, dropping barometric pressure, seasonal vitamin D loss, and air pollution all measurably affect inflammation levels. Here is what the science shows.

item.title: Can Inflammation Cause Weight Gain? What the Scale Won't Tell You
item.description: Chronic inflammation drives belly fat, insulin resistance, and leptin resistance. Learn why the scale misleads and how tracking inflammation can support your weight goals.

item.title: Why Inflammation May Be Sabotaging Your Weight Loss
item.description: Find out how hidden chronic inflammation can stall weight loss and disrupt your metabolism. Sensa helps you track CRP so you can address inflammation head-on.
